title: Collaboration App Permission and Consent Audit

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-generated from legacy markdown 'Regulations' line during the per-UC migration. Requires SME review to fill in version, clause, mode, and assurance for GDPR.

dataSources: App consent, OAuth grant, and permission change events
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="azure:audit"
| search (ActivityType="Consent to application" OR ActivityType="Add app role assignment")
| stats count by ApplicationId, ResourceDisplayName, UserPrincipalName
| sort -count
description: Third-party app consent and OAuth grants can expose data. Auditing consent and permission changes reduces shadow IT and abuse risk.
value: Third-party app consent and OAuth grants can expose data. Auditing consent and permission changes reduces shadow IT and abuse risk.
implementation: Ingest Entra ID consent and app role assignment events. Alert on new high-privilege consents or consent by non-admin. Report on app usage and permission scope.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices` (Entra ID), `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`, M365 audit.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: App consent, OAuth grant, and permission change events.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest Entra ID consent and app role assignment events. Alert on new high-privilege consents or consent by non-admin. Report on app usage and permission scope.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="azure:audit"
| search (ActivityType="Consent to application" OR ActivityType="Add app role assignment")
| stats count by ApplicationId, ResourceDisplayName, UserPrincipalName
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Collaboration App Permission and Consent Audit** ‚Äî Third-party app consent and OAuth grants can expose data. Auditing consent and permission changes reduces shadow IT and abuse risk.

Documented **Data sources**: App consent, OAuth grant, and permission change events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices` (Entra ID), `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`, M365 audit. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: azure:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype="azure:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ApplicationId, ResourceDisplayName, UserPrincipalName** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (consent events), Bar chart (apps by consent count), Timeline (consent over time).
visualization: Table (consent events), Bar chart (apps by consent count), Timeline (consent over time).
